preamble: The close-mid front unrounded vowel [e] lowers to the open-mid front unrounded vowel [ɛ], a common vowel lowering process occurring in stressed syllables, before certain consonants, or as part of broader vowel shift chains. This process is widely attested across Germanic and Romance language families.

examples.note: Lowering of close-mid vowels in stressed position before certain consonants, particularly alveolars and velars.
Lowering of Latin [e] in certain phonological contexts during the development to Old Romance languages.
Dialectal lowering of close-mid front vowels in open syllables and before consonant clusters.
Allophonic lowering of /e/ before consonants in word-final position and in rapid speech.

context: Before certain consonants (dentals, velars) in Spanish; stressed position lowering
allophones_note: Allophonic lowering of /e/ in Spanish is conditioned by following consonant and stress
